name: loadCalculation
invariants: output.ok === false || output.result.circuits === undefined || output.result.circuits.every((c) => Number.isFinite(c.amps) && c.amps >= 0)
_note: Pure compute, reads artifact.data.circuits (NecCodeCalc sends runDomain('electrical','loadCalculation',{input:{artifact:{data}}}) → dispatch peels the redundant wrapper → handler reads artifact.data.circuits). amps = watts/voltage; breaker/wire sized at 125% per NEC 310.16/240.4. Fail-closed: poisoned watts/voltage sanitise via num() — non-finite → 0/120, negatives clamp to 0, so amps/totals never leak NaN/Infinity and a load is never negative (safety-relevant). Empty/missing circuits returns the honest 'Add circuits' prompt, not a fabricated tally. Result cards render circuits[].{name,amps,breakerSize,wireGauge} + totalWatts/totalAmps/panelSizeRecommended/utilization/safetyMargin/nec80PercentRule EXACTLY (field-aligned against PanelLoadCalc 2026-06-28). No STATE, no DB.
